compiled_state.title: [test-doc-migration.sh]: Scanning for legacy CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/ refs -> shim-exempt annotated lines counted as violations
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
Lines annotated with `# shim-exempt: <reason>` in plugin skill/workflow files should be excluded from the legacy-ref count, consistent with how check-shim-refs.sh treats them.

## Actual Behavior
test_no_legacy_plugin_root_refs fails with COUNT=3. The grep chain does not exclude shim-exempt lines. Three files are flagged:
- plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/behavioral-testing-standard.md:141 (illustrative example)
- plugins/dso/skills/interface-contracts/SKILL.md:184 (example in checklist)
- plugins/dso/skills/resolve-conflicts/SKILL.md:110 (post-merge step)

All three lines carry # shim-exempt annotations.

## Fix
Either add `grep -v '# shim-exempt'` to the exclusion chain in test-doc-migration.sh, or update the flagged files to use the .claude/scripts/dso shim in their examples.
